responseMessageContents.text: I'm looking at the file structure—254 total files in the expo/app directory with around 230 TypeScript screen components, and I need to organize these into a numbered breakdown grouped by functional area to show the user what their app can do end-to-end. Continuing through the rest of the root-level screens and organizing them into logical sections with continuous numbering — auth flows, investment features, communication tools, admin dashboards, and utility pages. This will be comprehensive but matches what was requested.

Here's the full end-to-end breakdown, pulled live from your app's code just now. Every screen counted and grouped — continuous numbering:

## Main App — 5 Tabs (8 screens)

1. Home dashboard
2. Chat tab
3. Market tab (live property market)
4. Portfolio tab (your holdings)
5. Profile tab
6. Invest hub
7. Submit property (invest flow)
8. Debt acquisition / Land partner / Profit tools (invest flows)

## Sign Up & Identity (12)

9. Login · 10. Signup · 11. Reset password · 12. Member register · 13. Enterprise register · 14. Owner login · 15. Owner signup · 16. Owner access · 17. KYC verification · 18. Authenticator (2FA) · 19. Personal info · 20. Security settings

## Investing Engine (14)

21. Buy shares · 22. Sell shares · 23. Gift shares · 24. Auto-reinvest · 25. Copy investing · 26. Smart investing · 27. Compare investments · 28. Resale marketplace · 29. JV invest · 30. JV agreement · 31. JV architecture · 32. Property detail page · 33. Property documents · 34. Title review

## Money & Records (7)

35. Wallet · 36. Statements · 37. Tax documents · 38. Tax info · 39. IPX Earn · 40. VIP tiers · 41. Waitlist

## Apply / Partner Flows (4)

42. Agent apply · 43. Broker apply · 44. Influencer apply · 45. Contract generator

## Communication (11)

46. Chat hub · 47. Chat room · 48. Email inbox · 49. Email compose · 50. Email detail · 51. SMS compose · 52. SMS dashboard · 53. SMS history · 54. SMS reports · 55. Send test email · 56. Send test SMS

## Growth & Marketing (10)

57. Referrals · 58. Viral growth · 59. Share content · 60. Business card · 61. QR code · 62. Investor pitch · 63. Investor prospectus · 64. Video presentation · 65. Landing page · 66. AI gallery

## Info, Legal & Trust (13)

67. Company info · 68. Legal · 69. Trust center · 70. Language · 71. Notifications · 72. Notification settings · 73. App guide · 74. App demo · 75. Search · 76. Activation center · 77. V1 brief · 78. Global intelligence · 79. Opportunity intelligence

## Reports & System (12)

80. Analytics report · 81. AI automation report · 82. App report · 83. Backend audit · 84. Registration audit · 85. System health · 86. System blueprint · 87. Live evidence dashboard · 88. Live operations center · 89. Developer breakdown · 90. Supabase export · 91. Auto-repair · 92. API list

## Admin Panel (60 screens)

93–152. Full staff back-office: dashboard, control tower, members (list + detail), properties, applications, team, staff activity, fees, investor profits, JV deals, land partners, title companies, lender directory/search/sync, banners, broadcast, marketing, growth, engagement, retargeting, social command, AI outreach, AI video, outreach analytics, lead intelligence, email engine/inbox/accounts/management, landing control/analytics/submissions, feature control, owner controls, audit log, registration audit, quality control, sync diagnostics, system map, system monitor, traffic control, data recovery, image backup, supabase scripts, publication log, API keys, app docs, developer handoff, IVX agent jobs, IVX developer workspace/actions, auth debug, deploy waitlist, admin chat room, intro

## IVX Owner Command Center (61 screens)

153–213. Your private AI-powered HQ: owner control center, CTO dashboard, executive layer, capital command center, capital network/outreach/pipeline, investor discovery, investors, deal matching, deal tracking, lead scoring, master lead list, lead audit log, CRM import, contact detail, campaign, outreach, inbox, Gmail provider, AI chat, opportunity engine, research lab, innovation dashboard, project dashboard, daily report, business impact, autonomous activity/scale, live coding stream, live work, developer monitor, deploy, GitHub sync, diagnostics, production diagnostics, auth diagnostics, AI proxy status, incidents, durability proof, proof ledger, proof test, enterprise audit log, security box, user permissions, access requests, invite staff/role, role dashboard, owner AI log, owner ordering, power tools, runtime variables, variables, files, search, independence, Rork independence

## Built-in API Routes (9)

214–222. Assistant AI, auth lookup, deploy bridge, owner bootstrap, plan creator, owner AI, owner variables, members, access — server routes running inside the app

## Totals (verified live)

```text
Screen & route files in expo/app:  254
Actual screens (excl. layouts):   ~220
Backend API modules:               111
Backend services:                  273
Reusable components:                51
```

End to end: a member can **sign up → verify KYC → browse properties → buy/sell/gift shares → track portfolio → earn referrals → get statements & tax docs**, staff run everything from the **admin panel**, and you command it all from the **IVX owner center** with AI outreach, capital pipeline, and live system monitoring.

Want me to turn this into a shareable one-page document (PDF-style screen map) for investors or your team?
